Student Submission:


The submissions for this assignment are posts in the assignment's discussion. Below are the discussion posts for Lorena Ramos Rodriguez, or you can view the full discussion.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 4, 2025 6:10PM
Lorena Ramos Rodriguez
In the short story ‘A Worn Path’ by Eudora Welty, the main character Phoenix Jackson, is both symbolic and literal element. Phoenix Jackson, a very old fragile women, takes a journey walk along a very dark woodsy path to get to the hospital to pick up medication for her very ill grandson.

Along her journey she encounters a hunter who helps her out of ditch, a poor old women fell into a ditch, but she didn’t die and called out for help. She was not going to let the ground of nature defeat her. Once the hunter, who was white, approached her with a gun and his dog, it is thought that he might shoot her or let her rot in the ditch, clearly that was not the case. This path as in the title, ‘A Worn Path’ symbolizes her perseverance to get to her end goal no matter what her situation or her state of health. She made it through, like in the story where it states, “Doesn’t the gun scare you?......No, sir, I seen plenty go off closer by, in my day, and for less than what I done.” Even though she knew that it was not safe to go through the woods or simply because she was a women of color she walked through no matter the situations.
